transformation_summary: Extracted title (Mr/Mrs/Miss/Master) from Name column using regex; encoded as integer category — encodes gender and social class implicitly, strong survival signal
Derived FamilySize from SibSp + Parch + 1; IsAlone flag; binned Age into 5 groups after median imputation — family size strongly predicts survival
Extracted deck letter from Cabin column; mapped to integer 0-8; filled missing Cabin as deck 0 — deck correlates with class and proximity to lifeboats
Extracted ticket prefix from Ticket column; imputed Embarked mode; created Fare-per-person by dividing Fare by FamilySize — reduces per-person cost confound
Log-transformed skewed numeric columns (amount, income); imputed missing values with column median — reduces effect of outliers on tree splits
Computed debt-to-income ratio and balance-to-credit-limit utilization; flagged accounts with any delinquency in past 2 years — these ratios are the strongest credit default predictors
Binned continuous credit features (age, income, balance) into quantile bins; creates monotonic ordinal encoding that captures non-linear relationships in tree models
One-hot encoded categorical loan purpose and employment type columns; removed rare categories (< 1% frequency) by grouping into 'Other' — prevents high-cardinality overfitting
Log-transformed target-correlated numeric features; created TotalSF from basement + first + second floor areas — floor area is the single strongest price predictor
Created house age from YrSold - YearBuilt; remodel age from YrSold - YearRemodAdd; quality score product OverallQual * OverallCond — age and quality interaction drives significant variance
Computed rooms-per-floor ratio and bathroom ratio; imputed missing LotFrontage with neighborhood median — neighborhood-level imputation outperforms global median for spatial data
Encoded ordinal quality features (ExterQual, KitchenQual, BsmtQual) as numeric scale Poor=1..Excellent=5; handles NA as 0 — ordinal encoding preserves ranking information lost in one-hot
Extracted text length and word count from headline; filled missing description with empty string — text length is a simple but effective proxy for article complexity

Computed uppercase ratio and punctuation density in headline text; extracted publication hour from date — uppercase and punctuation patterns distinguish news categories (e.g., politics vs. entertainment)
Hashed category-specific keywords as binary presence features; combined headline and description into a single text field for downstream TF-IDF — text combination reduces feature sparsity
Extracted author name length and whether author name contains a comma (last, first format) as features; filled missing authors — author format correlates with publication type
code:
def preprocess(df):
    import pandas as pd
    if 'authors' in df.columns:
        df['authors'] = df['authors'].fillna('')
        df['author_len'] = df['authors'].str.len().astype(int)
        df['has_comma_name'] = df['authors'].str.contains(',').astype(int)
    return df

transformation_summary: Created lag-1 and lag-7 features from target column; 7-day rolling mean and rolling std — lag and rolling features capture autocorrelation and weekly seasonality in time series regression
code:
def preprocess(df):
    import pandas as pd
    target_candidates = [c for c in df.columns if df[c].dtype in ['float64', 'int64'] and c != df.columns[0]]
    if target_candidates:
        col = target_candidates[0]
        df[f'{col}_lag1'] = df[col].shift(1)
        df[f'{col}_lag7'] = df[col].shift(7)
        df[f'{col}_roll7_mean'] = df[col].rolling(7).mean()
        df[f'{col}_roll7_std'] = df[col].rolling(7).std()
        df = df.dropna()
    return df

transformation_summary: Extracted hour-of-day, day-of-week, month, and is-weekend from datetime index — temporal cyclic features expose within-week and within-day demand patterns
code:
def preprocess(df):
    import pandas as pd
    date_cols = [c for c in df.columns if 'date' in c.lower() or 'time' in c.lower() or 'timestamp' in c.lower()]
    if date_cols:
        dt = pd.to_datetime(df[date_cols[0]], errors='coerce')
        df['hour'] = dt.dt.hour
        df['dayofweek'] = dt.dt.dayofweek
        df['month'] = dt.dt.month
        df['is_weekend'] = (dt.dt.dayofweek >= 5).astype(int)
    return df

transformation_summary: Applied min-max normalization to all numeric columns per feature; handles outlier-heavy time series where tree models benefit from normalized input ranges
Computed exponentially weighted moving average (span=7 and span=30) as additional features — EWMA downweights old observations and captures recent trend more effectively than simple rolling mean
code:
def preprocess(df):
    import pandas as pd
    numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='number').columns.tolist()
    if numeric_cols:
        col = numeric_cols[0]
        df[f'{col}_ewm7'] = df[col].ewm(span=7).mean()
        df[f'{col}_ewm30'] = df[col].ewm(span=30).mean()
    return df
